student_verification.name: AML Part B Student
student_verification.declaration: I confirm that all LLM interactions listed below are complete and accurate, and that all decisions regarding ablation choices, failure mode identification, and honest reflection are my own.

interactions.prompt: What are two good components to ablate in NBSVM that would be informative?
interactions.response_summary: Suggested (1) the NB log-count ratio feature scaling and (2) the interpolation parameter β.
interactions.student_modification: N/A — code was rewritten based on conceptual guidance only

interactions.prompt: How do I implement the ablated version of NBSVM without the NB ratio?
interactions.response_summary: Suggested passing X_train directly to SVM instead of X_train.multiply(r).
interactions.student_modification: Modified to keep the interpolation step (Eq. 4) so only ONE component is ablated at a time. Added docstring.

interactions.prompt: The ablation 1 result shows 50% accuracy — is that meaningful or a bug?
interactions.response_summary: Explained that 50% on binary classification = random guessing. Suggested it shows r is the primary discriminative source.
interactions.student_modification: N/A — code was rewritten based on conceptual guidance only

interactions.prompt: Help me format a line plot for the beta ablation showing both unigram and bigram curves.
interactions.response_summary: Suggested ax.plot with two series, axvline for paper default, grid for readability.
interactions.student_modification: Added annotations for β=0 and β=1 endpoints, adjusted y-axis range to show variation.
